ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Pudina Fish Tikka; closest parent 'fish food product' (FOODON_03302051) used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW (Fish, grilled) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for mint-marinated Indian grilled fish tikka.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for Pudina Fish Tikka as a specific prepared dish.

main_ingredients: fish (firm white fish such as basa, tilapia, or pomfret)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Pudina Fish Tikka consists of approximately 3 to 4 grilled fish pieces weighing around 200 grams, as commonly served in Indian restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Fish tikka / tandoori fish — nutrient composition adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods and USDA FoodData Central (Tilapia, cooked, dry heat, FDC ID 175177)
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Pudina Fish Tikka is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN or USDA. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for tandoori fish preparations and USDA FoodData Central cooked tilapia (FDC ID 175177), adjusted for the mint-yogurt marinade contribution. The marinade adds modest protein and fat from yogurt and oil; mint and spices contribute negligible macronutrients at typical quantities. Energy and macros reflect a composite estimate consistent with published Indian grilled fish tikka nutritional profiles in peer-reviewed dietary studies.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of protein-dominant grilled fish preparations — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for fish and high-protein low-carbohydrate cooked dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pudina Fish Tikka has no direct GI measurement in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 30 (low) based on the analogous category of high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled fish dishes. The University of Sydney GI Database consistently places plain and marinated grilled fish in the low GI range (GI < 35) due to negligible starch content and high protein content, which blunts postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (30 × 2.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 1.68, rounded to 1.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from marinade and salt
hypertension.guidance: Pudina Fish Tikka contains approximately 320 mg sodium per 100 g (640 mg per standard 200 g serving), which represents about 28% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and request reduced-salt preparation when possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low glycemic index of approximately 30 and a glycemic load of only 1.7 per serving, Pudina Fish Tikka is a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as the high protein content (19.5 g per 100 g) and minimal carbohydrate (2.8 g per 100 g) support stable postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean fish protein with moderate unsaturated fat profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pudina Fish Tikka provides a favourable fat profile with low saturated fat (1.4 g per 100 g) and a combined monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat content of 3.6 g per 100 g, along with omega-3 fatty acids from fish, supporting cardiovascular health when consumed as part of a balanced diet; sodium intake should still be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pudina Fish Tikka is an Indian grilled fish dish in which firm white fish — commonly basa, tilapia, or pomfret — is marinated in a vibrant blend of fresh mint (pudina), yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, lemon juice, and aromatic spices including cumin, coriander, and garam masala, then grilled at high heat in a tandoor oven or over charcoal until lightly charred on the exterior and moist and flaky within. The dish is characterised by a refreshing, herbaceous mint flavour balanced by the tang of yogurt and lemon, with a subtle heat from green chilli and warmth from the spice blend. The grilling process imparts a smoky aroma and a slightly crisp outer crust while preserving the delicate texture of the fish. Nutritionally, Pudina Fish Tikka is a high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish providing approximately 19.5 g protein and only 2.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or following low-glycaemic dietary patterns. Its low glycemic index (estimated GI 30) and low glycemic load (GL 1.7 per 200 g serving) further support blood glucose stability. The dish is naturally gluten-free and halal. Sodium content is moderate at approximately 320 mg per 100 g, which warrants attention for individuals with hypertension. In Singapore, Pudina Fish Tikka is served in Indian restaurants and is available through food delivery platforms such as field survey, typically as a starter or main course alongside naan, raita, or mint chutney.

confidence_reason: Pudina Fish Tikka is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN tandoori fish data and USDA cooked tilapia (FDC ID 175177) with adjustment for the mint-yogurt marinade. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for grilled fish. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match exists in a primary approved source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (tandoori fish preparations) and USDA FoodData Central cooked tilapia (FDC ID 175177); no exact entry for Pudina Fish Tikka exists in any approved primary source. 2. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as grilled fish with yogurt marinade contains no industrially produced trans fats; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for the analogous preparation. 3. GI is estimated at 30 (low) based on analogous grilled fish entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Pudina Fish Tikka was found. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 2.1 g from USDA tilapia data), polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 1.5 g from USDA tilapia data. All micronutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central tilapia and ICMR-NIN fish data and should be treated as indicative. 5. The field dataed in the source record (Anjana Kitchen – Six Battery Road, field survey data) is noted for provenance tracing only; field survey is not used as a nutritional source. 6. Serving size of 200 g is an estimate for a typical restaurant portion of 3–4 fish tikka pieces; actual restaurant portions may vary. 7. Halal status is assumed based on the typical preparation of this dish in Indian Muslim and North Indian restaurant contexts in Singapore; individual restaurant certification should be verified.
